title: ericameria parishii var. peninsularis
intro: Ericameria parishii var. peninsularis, also known as California island or island goldenbush, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Channel Islands of California. This shrub-like plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ericameria parishii var. peninsularis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of conditions, including skin irritations and wounds.
